description: Five-story batch fixing voice enrollment misses, silent audio-stack failures, and noisy calendar-sync errors uncovered during the 2026-04-17 Anu meeting post-mortem. Restores named voiceprints on scheduled meetings, makes audio health visible before recording, and eliminates silent-swallow paths.

userStories.title: Fix calendar-sync $setOnInsert error spam
userStories.description: As a developer, I want calendar polling to persist events to the local NeDB without throwing every 5 minutes so app.log is clean and events are actually available in the DB for downstream features.
acceptanceCriteria: Locate the syncCalendarEventsToDb call path (likely src/main/database.ts and/or src/main/calendar-watcher.ts) where $setOnInsert is used with updateAsync+upsert
Replace the $setOnInsert upsert with a find-then-insert-or-update pattern: findOneAsync by id; if present updateAsync({_id}, {$set: doc}); if absent insertAsync(doc)
After 3 consecutive polls with the same event set, the calendar events collection count stays stable (idempotent)
No lines matching 'ERROR calendar-sync' or '$setOnInsert' appear in app.log during a 10-minute run
Existing in-memory getUpcomingEvents() behavior is unchanged
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Fix voiceprint playback silent failure in Settings
userStories.description: As a user, when I click Play on a voiceprint row in Settings I want to hear the clip or see a visible error, not silently nothing.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit src/renderer/Settings.tsx playAudio (around line 625-649): chain .catch() on audio.play() and surface the rejection reason in the row's UI state

Restructure so audio creation and audio.play() happen without an await in between (to preserve the Chromium user-gesture chain): set audio.src after creation, call audio.play() synchronously inside the click handler, then let oncanplay trigger playback if needed — OR pre-fetch the blob once on mount of the voiceprint list
Clicking Play on Shravani Vatti or Zee (existing rows in voiceprints.db) produces audible playback
If the IPC returns an object with no audioClip, the Play button is disabled or labeled '(no audio)' rather than silently doing nothing
If audio.play() rejects, the row shows an inline error containing the rejection name or message for at least 3 seconds
Clicking Play on the currently-playing row stops it (existing toggle behavior preserved)
No changes to the IPC handler at src/main/main.ts:1033-1051 (it already handles the three NeDB audioClip shapes correctly)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open Settings, click Play on Shravani Vatti, confirm audio plays

userStories.title: Add calendar:active-event IPC and auto-attach calendarEventId when starting manual recordings
userStories.description: As a user, when I click Record Meeting during a scheduled calendar event, I want that recording to be linked to the event so attendees drive speaker labeling and voice enrollment.
acceptanceCriteria: Add IPC handler 'calendar:active-event' in src/main/main.ts that returns the single event from calendarWatcher.getUpcomingEvents() whose startTime <= now() AND (endTime || startTime + 90min) >= now()
Expose it on the preload bridge (src/main/preload.ts) as inwiseAPI.getActiveCalendarEvent()

In src/renderer/Sidebar.tsx Record Meeting button handler: call getActiveCalendarEvent() before emitting recording:audio-data; if a non-null event is returned, include its id as calendarEventId in the IPC payload
If the user hasn't typed a title and an active event is found, default the meeting title to the event's title
With a synthetic active calendar event (attendees = [user, other]), recording end-to-end produces a voice-enroll:auto log line and voiceprints.db gains a row named for the other attendee
With no active event, recording still works as ad-hoc (no regression)
Typecheck passes

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: simulate an in-progress calendar event, click Record Meeting, stop after a few seconds, confirm the title auto-filled and app.log shows calendarEventId was passed

userStories.title: Emit audio-health events from Badge and render indicator on Record Meeting pill
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see at a glance whether my mic and system audio are being captured correctly, without opening the Badge overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a shared audio-health channel: Badge.tsx sends IPC 'audio:health' with payload { micOk: boolean, systemAudioOk: boolean, message?: string } on every getUserMedia/desktopCapturer success or failure
Main process forwards the latest health state to all renderer windows (or exposes a pull IPC inwiseAPI.getAudioHealth())
Sidebar.tsx subscribes and renders a small amber dot on the Record Meeting pill when micOk=false or systemAudioOk=false
Hovering the dot shows a tooltip with the message (e.g. 'System audio unavailable — only your voice will be recorded')
Pill shows the same indicator during recording (Stop Recording state)
No visual change when both are ok
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open app with mic blocked at OS level, confirm amber indicator and tooltip on Record Meeting pill

userStories.title: Fire desktop Notification on audio capture failure
userStories.description: As a user, if my mic or system audio fails during recording, I want a desktop notification so I find out immediately — not after the meeting.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/main/main.ts, subscribe to the audio:health channel from US-004
When micOk transitions from true->false or systemAudioOk transitions from true->false during an active recording, fire new Notification({title, body}).show() if Notification.isSupported()
Debounce: do not fire more than once per 60 seconds for the same failure type
Notification body clearly describes the failure and the consequence (e.g. 'System audio lost — only your mic will be transcribed for the rest of this meeting')
No notification fires outside of an active recording
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add global unhandled-rejection handler and audit .play() call sites
userStories.description: As a developer, I want every audio playback call site to either catch its rejection or be covered by a global handler so future silent-swallow bugs can't hide.
acceptanceCriteria: Register window.addEventListener('unhandledrejection', ...) in the renderer entry (src/renderer/App.tsx or equivalent) that forwards the reason to main via IPC 'renderer:unhandled-rejection'
Main-process handler logs via the existing logger at error level with the reason.name and reason.message
Audit every .play() call in src/renderer/ (grep for '\.play\(' and 'new Audio'): each must be chained with .catch() OR wrapped in try/catch inside an async function
Settings.tsx, Badge.tsx, Onboarding.tsx, and TranscriptReviewModal.tsx play() sites are all verified safe
Manually point one play() at an invalid data URL temporarily, run the app, confirm app.log captures the rejection — then revert
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
